text: AI is increasingly common in the modern workplace. One major role is automation, where machines handle repetitive tasks like data entry, allowing people to focus on more complex work. Productivity tools, such as smart scheduling or writing assistants, also help workers save time and reduce errors. However, true potential lies in human-AI collaboration. Humans provide creativity and judgment, while AI processes large amounts of data quickly. This partnership is already changing some jobs, requiring new skills like managing these tools or interpreting AI results. Rather than replacing all jobs, AI often shifts them, creating roles that mix technical knowledge with human insight.
